question: Does the image depict a Cartesian coordinate system with an ellipse drawn on it?
Is the ellipse oriented horizontally (major axis along X) with correct proportions (wider than it is tall, consistent with a=4, b=3)?
Are the major axis endpoints labeled A₁ and A₂ at approximately (±4, 0), and minor axis endpoints labeled B₁ and B₂ at approximately (0, ±3)?
Are the two foci labeled F₁ and F₂ positioned on the X-axis inside the ellipse, with F₂ on the positive side (right)?
Is there a line segment PQ drawn passing through the right focus (F₂) with a slope of approximately 1 (45-degree angle)?
Is there a perpendicular line segment drawn from the origin (O) to the line PQ?
Is the perpendicular line segment from the origin rendered as a dashed line?
Is the intersection of the perpendicular and line PQ labeled 'H' (foot), and the intersection with the ellipse labeled 'R'?
Is there a visual right-angle symbol marking the intersection at point H?
Are coordinate tuples (x, y) annotated near the key points (A, B, F, P, Q, R, H, O)?
Is the length of segment PQ explicitly labeled or annotated on the diagram?
Is the area formula for triangle PQR explicitly written on the diagram?
